key_findings_for_synthesis.icp_candidate_a_tight: VP Engineering / Head of AI/ML / CTO at Brazilian fintechs (Nubank, Stone, Inter, XP, BTG, PagSeguro), revenue $500M+ or $1B+ valuation, AI-native Series B+ startups
key_findings_for_synthesis.icp_candidate_b_loose: Tier-1 Brazilian enterprises (banks, retailers, telcos) — buyer role unspecified in inputs, 'innovation lab' is known graveyard
key_findings_for_synthesis.outbound_recommendation: Decline broad outbound; prescribe NARROW Cold Calling 2.0 into Candidate A only with all 4 specifics + expected response rate 8-12%
key_findings_for_synthesis.sequencing: Q2 2026 foundations (LGPD/BRL/Portuguese/local AE+SE/telemetry) → Q3 2026 Seeds + narrow Spears pilot → Q4 2026 conditional enterprise motion based on Q3 data → Q1 2027 LATAM expansion decision based on Brazil ratios

item_scores.reasoning: Sophisticated two-candidate analysis. Candidate A (Brazilian fintechs) named at firmographic ($500M+ revenue or $1B+ valuation, named logos), behavioral (running multi-vendor pilots, English-fluent, Bedrock/API telemetry observable), trigger (Bedrock spend threshold OR public AI announcement OR competitor pilot to production). Buyer role: VP Engineering / Head of AI/ML / CTO. Candidate B (enterprise) explicitly named as loose: 'Inputs do not support tight specification of who signs.' Resolution required before broad outbound. What would resolve: 'identification of the specific function that owns production AI infra budget at 2-3 named banks, and evidence of at least one Brazilian-bank pilot reaching production on any frontier LLM.'

Absent — all GTM roles confirmed missing in Brazil (no SDR, AE, AM on the ground, no named Brazil sales leader). Plus latent risk identification: 'Founder/HQ-doing-sales risk is latent rather than acute. The risk activates the moment the first Brazil hire is made. If that hire is a generalist Country Manager expected to prospect, close, and farm flagship banks simultaneously, Ross's role-specialization principle is violated on day one.' Forward-looking diagnostic.

All three sources named with sophisticated diagnosis: Seeds weak and not owned (Anthropic doesn't control AWS-mediated referrals), Nets dominant by default not by design (self-serve API + 'AWS Bedrock is a Net Anthropic rents from a partner'), Spears zero. Plus key insight: 'The Nets themselves are leaking because the brand and language layer is wrong for the market' (Portuguese defaults to ChatGPT).

Hybrid path: declines broad outbound AND prescribes narrow outbound simultaneously. Hits 3 of 5 'when NOT to apply Spears' conditions: Nets still scaling and leaking, ICP not tight for enterprise, Seeds not yet built. Names what must be true before broad Spears: LGPD attestation, BRL pricing, Portuguese collateral, named AE for handoff, in-segment reference customer. PLUS prescribes narrow Spears into Candidate A with all 4 specifics: buyer (VP Engineering / Head of AI Platform at named fintechs), channel (email primary English-acceptable, LinkedIn secondary), trigger (Bedrock/API usage threshold OR public AI announcement OR competitor pilot news), message shape (technical, workload-specific, in-segment reference, named solutions engineer not demo). Plus expected response rate 8-12%. Exemplary execution combining both rubric paths.

6 specific ratios named with ranges and Brazilian-specific context: Bedrock/API self-serve to sales-qualified account (currently invisible because AWS owns relationship), outbound emails-to-response (5-15% on Candidate A), response-to-qualified-opp (10-25%), qualified-opp-to-close (15-30% per Ross, expect lower in year-one Brazilian enterprise), pilot-to-production (Brazilian-enterprise-specific ratio), ARR per closed account by segment. Frames Q2-Q3 2026 as 'diagnostic infrastructure quarter' — apparatus that decides whether to scale Spears, double Seeds, or fix Nets in Q4.

6 modes flagged with cited input evidence: lead-source blindness (Anthropic doesn't see/own/instrument largest Brazilian lead source per AWS Bedrock signal), premature outbound prescription risk (cites expansion thesis pressure to sign 'flagship Brazilian enterprise customers'), no conversion math (cited by absence in inputs), spray-and-pray risk on enterprise ICP (cites 'multi-vendor pilots; few have standardized'), role confusion latent (cites 'local sales and customer success presence' bundled phrase suggesting generalist hire likely), Net leakage (cites 'Portuguese-language LLM mentions default to ChatGPT').

4 quarters with explicit step-level dependencies. Q2 2026 foundations (5 numbered steps, 4 unblocking 5 unblocking everything downstream, 1-3 unblock procurement). Q3 2026 Seeds + tight Spears (step 7 produces references that step 8's outbound message needs). Q4 2026 conditional enterprise motion (step 9 requires steps 7+8 completed first). Q1 2027 LATAM expansion (don't start before Brazil pilot-to-production ratio known). Each dependency named with specific reasoning.

ICP 6/10 (calibrated lower because Candidate B is loose), Outbound 8/10 (Ross conditions hit cleanly). 3 specific open questions with binary triggers: any Brazilian bank moved frontier-LLM pilot to production on any vendor (yes shifts Q4 to Q3, no confirms innovation-lab graveyard), Bedrock-mediated vs direct ARR ratio (>70% Bedrock could invalidate direct-sales build), LGPD attestation + BRL pricing actually shipping in Q2 (slip cascades through downstream).
